spec_match.rationale: The route behavior is entirely simulated on the client side using JavaScript rather than hitting a real, wired route in server.ts as required by the repository rules.

interactive.rationale: Provides buttons to step through the simulated server branches, though it does not perform real network requests.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The index blurb accurately summarizes the server-side branching concept shown in the simulator.

edge_cases.rationale: Covers the core scenarios of normal navigation, prefetch, and speculation rules prerender.

references.rationale: Links back to the feature index and the ChromeStatus entry.

design_system.rationale: The styles use the shared CSS variables from the design system correctly and maintain good contrast.

openQuestions.title: Wire real backend route in server.ts
openQuestions.detail: The server-tuning demo currently uses a client-side simulation. According to the project rules, HTTP/header features should wire a real route in server.ts and fetch it live to surface the actual request headers and server responses (e.g., skipping A/B tests, custom cache-control).

summary: Demonstrates how a server would branch its routing decisions based on the Sec-Purpose header, but the behavior is entirely simulated on the client-side and lacks a real backend route.
